definition: Represent each sample by its spectral-band vector and compute spectral angle, spectral angle cosine, or weighted spectral correlation similarity to the training set. This audits whether model performance depends on spectral-signature overlap rather than spatial or label metadata alone.
example_usage: python spectral_angle_similarity.py --train train_spectra.csv --eval eval_spectra.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --band-cols-prefix band_

citations.title: Weighted spectral correlation angle target detection method for land-based hyperspectral imaging

evidence.summary: The paper motivates spectral similarity under imaging-condition uncertainty in hyperspectral target detection.
It describes spectral angle, spectral angle cosine, and distance-based spectral similarity measures.
Weighted spectral correlation angle improves robustness to same-object different-spectrum uncertainty.

limitations: Spectral signatures require consistent band calibration, atmospheric correction, and reflectance normalization.
Spatial context can dominate some hyperspectral tasks and should be audited separately.
Band weighting can encode target knowledge and must be declared.
quality_gates: Report wavelength range, preprocessing, band alignment, normalization, and removed bands.
Run separate curves for spectral angle, spatial distance, acquisition condition, and sensor/domain when possible.
Check whether illumination or acquisition metadata explains the spectral similarity axis.
